channel.title: CodeCosts Blog
channel.description: AI coding tool news, pricing updates, and analysis. Compare GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code, and more.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Security Engineers 2026: Pentesting, SIEM, IaC Scanning & Compliance Guide
item.description: The security engineer's guide to AI coding tools in 2026. Every tool ranked for pentesting scripts, SIEM rule writing (Splunk SPL, KQL, Sigma), IaC security scanning, vulnerability research, and compliance-sensitive environments. The sensitivity problem, the dual-use problem, and domain-first recommendations from $0 to self-hosted.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Embedded & IoT Engineers 2026: STM32, ESP32, Zephyr & Bare-Metal Guide
item.description: The embedded engineer's guide to AI coding tools in 2026. Every tool ranked for bare-metal C, RTOS firmware, HAL libraries, Arduino, ESP-IDF, Zephyr, and register-level programming. The training data problem, the IDE problem, and platform-first recommendations from $0 to $40/mo.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Game Developers 2026: Unity, Unreal, Godot & Shader Guide
item.description: Game development AI tool guide for 2026. Every tool ranked for Unity C#, Unreal C++, Godot GDScript, HLSL/GLSL shaders, and engine-specific APIs. The engine problem, the shader problem, and engine-first recommendations from $0 to $40/mo.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Data Engineers 2026: Spark, dbt, Airflow & SQL Pipeline Guide
item.description: Data engineers work across 4-5 languages in a single pipeline. Every AI tool ranked for SQL dialect awareness, dbt Jinja templating, Airflow DAGs, PySpark, and Terraform. Pipeline-first recommendations from $0 to $40/mo.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Mobile Developers 2026: iOS, Android, Flutter & React Native Guide
item.description: The mobile developer's guide to AI coding tools in 2026. Organized by platform — iOS/Swift, Android/Kotlin, Flutter, React Native, .NET MAUI — because your IDE determines your options. Verified pricing, honest limitations, and platform-by-platform recommendations.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Students & Bootcamp Grads 2026: Free Tiers, Discounts & Smart Usage
item.description: Complete guide to free and discounted AI coding tools for students and bootcamp graduates in 2026. Every student discount verified, the $0 power stack, a research-backed learning stage framework, and what bootcamp grads need to know about portfolios and the new AI-assisted interview landscape.

item.title: AI Coding Tools vs Traditional IDEs: Is It Worth Switching in 2026?
item.description: Should you switch from VS Code, IntelliJ, Vim, or Emacs to an AI-powered coding tool? Honest tradeoffs, IDE-by-IDE migration guides, free tier comparison, cost-vs-time ROI analysis, and the hybrid approach most developers actually use.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Frontend Engineers 2026: React, Vue, Svelte & CSS Guide
item.description: The frontend engineer's guide to AI coding tools in 2026. Every tool ranked for React components, Vue composables, Svelte stores, CSS/Tailwind, accessibility, and testing. Framework-specific recommendations for React/Next.js, Vue/Nuxt, Svelte/SvelteKit, and Angular. The $0 to $40/mo stacks for every frontend workflow.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Backend Engineers 2026: APIs, Databases & Microservices Guide
item.description: The backend engineer's guide to AI coding tools in 2026. Every tool ranked for REST APIs, database queries, microservices, and testing. Framework-specific recommendations for Spring Boot, Django, Express, Go, and Rails. The $0 to $40/mo stacks for every backend workflow.

item.title: How to Write Better Prompts for AI Coding Tools (2026 Guide)
item.description: The practical guide to prompting AI coding assistants. 5 prompt templates that work everywhere, tool-specific strategies for Copilot/Cursor/Claude Code/Windsurf, rules file setup, context management, and quota-aware prompting.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for DevOps Engineers 2026: Terraform, Docker, K8s & CI/CD Guide
item.description: Most AI tool reviews test on Python and React. We tested on Terraform modules, Dockerfiles, Kubernetes manifests, and GitHub Actions. The $0 power stack, terminal-native agents for IaC, and the security checklist for AI-generated infrastructure.

item.title: How to Switch AI Coding Tools Without Losing Productivity (2026 Migration Guide)
item.description: Most developers switch AI coding tools the wrong way. The parallel-run method, config transfer checklists for Copilot/Cursor/Windsurf/Claude Code, keybinding maps, switching cost calculator, and when NOT to switch.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Mobile Developers 2026: iOS, Android & Cross-Platform Guide
item.description: Xcode has almost no AI plugin support. Android Studio has the best. Every tool ranked by platform: SwiftUI, Jetpack Compose, Flutter, React Native. The $0 stacks for each platform, IDE lock-in problem, and the $30/mo combo for cross-platform teams.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Data Scientists 2026: Jupyter, pandas & ML Pipeline Guide
item.description: Most AI coding tool reviews ignore data science workflows. Jupyter notebook support ranked, best tools for pandas/numpy/sklearn, ML pipeline recommendations, and the $0 to $40/mo stacks for every DS workflow.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Freelancers 2026: ROI Calculator, Client Work Setup & Tax Deductions
item.description: Every dollar spent on AI coding tools must generate more billable revenue. ROI framework for freelancers at every hourly rate, the $0 to $40/mo tool stacks, client IP and data privacy considerations, and tax deduction guidance.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Students 2026: Free Tiers, Education Discounts & Learning Paths
item.description: GitHub Copilot is completely free for verified students. JetBrains gives free IDE + AI access. Every free tier ranked for learning, the $0/month student power stack, and how to use AI tools without hurting your education.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Enterprise 2026: Compliance, Procurement & the 50+ Seat Playbook
item.description: The enterprise buyer's guide to AI coding tools. SSO, SOC 2, HIPAA, FedRAMP, IP indemnity, self-hosted options, and real cost modeling for 50-500+ developer teams.

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Startups 2026: The Complete Guide from Bootstrap to Series A
item.description: How to pick AI coding tools at every startup stage. Real cost modeling from $0 to $19/seat, IP indemnity for fundraising, when to standardize, and the stacks that maximize developer output per dollar.

item.title: GitHub Copilot vs Claude Code 2026: IDE Plugin vs Terminal Agent
item.description: Copilot is $10/mo with fast autocomplete in every IDE. Claude Code is $20/mo with a terminal-native autonomous agent. Full pricing comparison, feature breakdown, team costs, and the $30/mo combo strategy that many devs are using.

item.title: Windsurf vs GitHub Copilot 2026: 40+ IDEs and Compliance vs GitHub's Ecosystem
item.description: Windsurf is now $20/mo — double Copilot's $10. But Windsurf supports 40+ IDEs, has HIPAA/FedRAMP/ITAR compliance, and its own SWE-1.5 model. Full comparison of pricing, billing models, IDE support, and compliance requirements.

item.title: Best Free AI Coding Tool in 2026: Every Free Tier Ranked
item.description: We ranked every free AI coding tool in 2026. Gemini Code Assist offers 180,000 free completions vs Copilot's 2,000. Amazon Q includes free security scans. Cody has unlimited autocomplete. Complete ranking with the optimal $0/month power stack.

item.title: Cursor vs Windsurf 2026: Both $20/mo Now — Which AI Code Editor Wins?
item.description: Windsurf killed credits, raised prices to $20/mo, and moved to daily quotas. Now at price parity with Cursor, the comparison changes: credits vs quotas, Composer vs Cascade, VS Code fork vs 40+ IDEs, and compliance certifications.

item.title: GitHub Copilot vs Cursor 2026: The Real Cost Comparison
item.description: Copilot costs $10/mo and Cursor costs $20/mo — but the real price depends on how you code. Premium request multipliers, credit pools, and unlimited Auto mode change the math. Full breakdown for light users, power users, and teams.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for Dart & Flutter Developers (2026)
item.description: Flutter is the only major framework where the maker also controls the AI model and IDE. We compared Gemini, Antigravity, Claude Code, Cursor, and Copilot for widget trees, state management, the MCP server, and the three-way IDE decision.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for Ruby & Rails Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for Ruby and Rails: ActiveRecord, RSpec/Minitest, RubyMine vs VS Code, Shopify-scale patterns, metaprogramming, dynamic typing challenges, and gem ecosystem support. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for C# & .NET Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for C# and .NET: LINQ, async/await, Entity Framework Core, ASP.NET Core, Blazor, MAUI, and Visual Studio integration. Microsoft built C#, Visual Studio, and Copilot — does that triple lock-in mean Copilot wins by default? Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for Kotlin & Android Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for Kotlin: Jetpack Compose, coroutines and Flow, Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP), Gradle configuration, Room/SQLDelight, and Android Studio compatibility. JetBrains built both Kotlin and the IDE — does that home-field advantage translate? Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for Swift & iOS Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for Swift: SwiftUI, UIKit, Xcode integration, async/await, CoreData/SwiftData, XCTest, and App Store workflows. The Xcode lock-in problem makes Swift unique — only Copilot works inside Xcode. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for PHP Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for PHP: WordPress plugin development, Laravel/Symfony support, legacy code migration (PHP 5→8), Composer, PHPUnit, and PhpStorm compatibility. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for TypeScript Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for TypeScript: generics, conditional types, mapped types, type guards, Zod schema inference, tRPC, strict mode compliance, and declaration files. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for C++ Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for C++: template metaprogramming, RAII and smart pointers, move semantics, CMake integration, Unreal Engine support, and undefined behavior detection. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for Rust Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for Rust: ownership and borrowing, lifetime annotations, unsafe code, async/tokio patterns, WASM support, and cargo integration. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for Go Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for Go: goroutine handling, interface generation, error wrapping, Kubernetes/cloud-native code, table-driven tests, and protobuf/gRPC. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for Java Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for Java: Spring Boot, Maven/Gradle, IntelliJ IDEA support, JUnit, enterprise readiness, and Java version upgrade automation. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: How Much Does AI Coding Actually Cost? Interactive 2026 Calculator
item.description: Interactive calculator to estimate real AI coding costs. Input team size, usage level, and feature needs — get personalized annual estimates for Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, and every major tool.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for JavaScript/TypeScript Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for JavaScript and TypeScript: advanced types, React/Next.js support, monorepo awareness, JSX completions, and build tools. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Best AI Coding Tool for Python Developers (2026)
item.description: Ranked comparison of AI coding tools for Python: type inference, Django/FastAPI support, Jupyter, pytest, and data science libraries. Tool-by-tool breakdown with cost analysis by workflow.

item.title: Cursor Pro vs Business: Is the Upgrade Worth It for Your Team?
item.description: Cursor Pro costs $20/mo. Business costs $40/seat. The AI is identical — we compare admin tools, SSO, compliance, team costs, and tell you when Business is worth the 2x premium.

item.title: Claude Code vs Cursor for AI Agents: Which Is Better for Autonomous Coding?
item.description: Terminal agent vs IDE agent. Full comparison of Claude Code and Cursor agent features, pricing, parallel execution, and use cases.

item.title: GitHub Copilot Pro vs Pro+: Is the $39/mo Plan Worth It?
item.description: Pro costs $10/mo with 300 premium requests. Pro+ costs $39/mo with 1,500. Break-even math, feature comparison, and clear recommendation.

item.title: Cheapest AI Coding Tool in 2026: Cost-Per-Feature Breakdown
item.description: We compared every AI coding tool by cost per feature — completions, chat, agent mode. Here's which tool gives the most value per dollar in 2026.

item.title: Cursor Now Works in JetBrains IDEs + Self-Hosted Agents for Enterprise
item.description: Cursor expanded to JetBrains via ACP, launched self-hosted cloud agents, and shipped Composer 2. Full breakdown of what changed and what it costs.

item.title: Gemini Code Assist Free Tier: 90x More Completions Than Copilot Free
item.description: 180,000 vs 2,000 completions per month. Full comparison of Gemini Code Assist Free and GitHub Copilot Free for 2026.

item.title: GitHub Copilot Will Train on Your Code by Default — How to Opt Out
item.description: Starting April 24, GitHub will use Free, Pro, and Pro+ user data to train AI models. Here's how to opt out before the deadline.

item.title: Windsurf Kills Credits, Moves to Quotas — Developer Backlash Explained
item.description: Windsurf replaced its credit system with daily/weekly quotas and raised Pro from $15 to $20/mo. Full breakdown and comparison.

item.title: The Hidden Costs of AI Coding Tools: What the Pricing Page Won't Tell You
item.description: Premium request limits, overage charges, seat minimums, and the gotchas that turn a $20/month tool into a $50+ expense.

item.title: AI Coding Tools: What Changed in March 2026
item.description: Monthly roundup: Copilot agent mode, Cursor Ultra, Windsurf enterprise, Claude Code updates, and what it means for your wallet.
